title: The Knight of the Tower
summary: The repository artifact is a pinned copy of a 1997 University of Michigan encoding made from Thomas Wright's 1906 revised EETS edition. Wright's text is composite: it principally transcribes British Library Harley MS 1764, the only known witness to an anonymous earlier Middle English translation, but uses William Caxton's distinct later translation for a small opening repair, two internal lacunae, and the entire missing ending. Matching Thomas Lane ownership inscriptions printed by Wright with Seymour de Ricci's census identifies British Library IB.55085 as the Caxton copy used. The U-M encoding preserves the edition title page, Furnivall foreword, Wright introduction, pp. 1-205, and many footnotes, while omitting the contents and back matter. Complete public scans of both Wright editions and a complete 127-canvas British Library IIIF facsimile of Harley MS 1764 are cached privately. The IIIF manifest asserts that its images are public domain in most countries other than the United Kingdom; its linked reuse page currently returns 404, so UK status and any provider permission requirements remain unresolved rather than overriding that non-UK assertion.
review_notes: The target work is the anonymous earlier Middle English translation. Geoffroy authored the French source, and Caxton translated a separate later English version.
Harley MS 1764 lacks leaves after fols. 24 and 32 and ends imperfectly at fol. 54v. Caxton passages in Wright are substitutes, not reconstructed anonymous wording.
The large Caxton-derived spans are printed pp. 72-75, 99-101, and 167-205; a small opening repair on p. 1 is also marked.
CERL's legacy digital link concerns the parallel copy C.11.c.6 = IB.55086, not Wright's IB.55085, and was unavailable when checked.
Michels-Kohlhage reports EEBO images from IB.55085. The subscription record was not directly authenticated, so it is modeled at medium confidence.

The Harley ZIP contains 127 provider-native full images. Although 1800 pixels wide was requested, the helper avoided upscaling and retrieved full images generally about 2000 pixels high with variable width.
The XML header says front matter and notes were omitted, but the file preserves a title page, two front divisions, and 102 NOTE1 elements. Only the observed omissions are asserted.
The CME repository Unlicense applies to contributor-controlled repository material; it does not automatically override inherited U-M, British Library, or Internet Archive terms.

notes: Rossiter copied Harley MS 1764 and inserted missing passages from Caxton.
Brock compiled a thirteen-page Additions and Corrections list for errors in the first edition.
Fox checked new proofs through p. 48 against Harley MS 1764; Furnivall checked the remainder, added French-source notes, and wrote the foreword.
Munro supplied the contents, fresh notes, glossary, phrases and proverbs, and subject index. The U-M encoding omits the contents and back matter.

physical_description.extent: ff. 1* + 54, plus three unfoliated modern paper flyleaves at the beginning and end
physical_description.format: Codex, mostly gatherings of eight, written in two columns
physical_description.dimensions: 315 x 215 mm; text space 220 x 160 mm
notes: Gothic cursive script; decorated initials and one rubbed historiated initial on f. 1r.
The manuscript lacks leaves after fols. 24 and 32 and is incomplete at the end.

entities.label: British Library IB.55085, Caxton's 1484 Knight of the Tower
entities.description: A nearly perfect copy of Caxton's distinct English translation. Thomas Lane inscriptions identify it as Wright's Caxton source. The scholarly_edition class is used technically for this historical printed witness.

rights.component: Contributor-controlled repository material in the pinned CME repository
rights.jurisdiction: Worldwide, to the extent the contributor controls the material
rights.basis: The pinned CME repository contains an Unlicense dedication and license grant.
notes: This statement does not override rights or contractual terms inherited from U-M, the British Library, Internet Archive, or other upstream providers.

rights.component: The KntTour-L digital text and materials presented by U-M
rights.basis: The current item and collection pages label the material Public Domain and use the No Copyright-United States statement.
notes: No Copyright-United States is jurisdiction-specific and is not an affirmative worldwide license.

rights.component: The imported U-M KntTour-L XML content as preserved in the repository artifact
rights.basis: The current U-M item labels the digital text Public Domain / No Copyright-United States, while the imported 1997 header retains a provider notice that expressly forbids redistribution by commercial and nonprofit content providers.
notes: The repository Unlicense record is intentionally narrower and must not be read as licensing this inherited content.
U-M clarification is needed before public redistribution of the imported encoding.

rights.component: The availability notice embedded in the 1997 XML header
rights.basis: The XML permits personal, research, and teaching copies with the notice and permits links, including by for-profit organizations, but says redistribution by commercial or nonprofit content providers is expressly forbidden.
notes: This legacy notice conflicts in practical effect with the current public-domain statement; the manifest records both without deciding which governs a planned redistribution.

rights.component: The 1906 edition's editorial text and paratext
rights.basis: The edition was published in 1906 and is outside United States copyright term.
notes: This conclusion concerns the historical edition, not necessarily the hosted scan files or provider metadata.

rights.component: Internet Archive PDF, OCR, scan-data, and metadata files
rights.basis: The cached Internet Archive metadata did not provide an item-specific license that could be carried into this manifest.

rights.jurisdiction: United States and jurisdictions where the medieval work's term has expired

rights.component: The JPEG images supplied through the British Library IIIF manifest
rights.jurisdiction: Most countries other than the United Kingdom (British Library manifest wording)
rights.basis: The IIIF manifest explicitly states that the digitized content is public domain in most countries other than the UK.
notes: The linked reuse page returned HTTP 404 on 2026-07-15; that link failure does not erase the manifest's non-UK public-domain assertion.

The current British Library website terms separately restrict some reuse, including commercial exploitation and recirculation; whether those terms bind reuse of these IIIF files requires case-specific review.

rights.component: The JPEG images supplied through the British Library IIIF manifest
rights.basis: The manifest excludes the UK from its public-domain assertion and directs users to an image-reuse page that currently returns 404.
notes: Contact licensing@bl.uk before UK publication or uses outside clearly documented permissions.

rights.component: New reproduction files supplied by a British Library imaging order
rights.basis: The digitisation service supplies reproductions for a fee, while the website directs licensing questions to the British Library; no order-specific license was obtained in this review.

rights.component: Caxton's 1483 English translation and 1484 printed text
rights.jurisdiction: United States and jurisdictions where the fifteenth-century work's term has expired
rights.basis: The translation was completed in 1483 and the book printed in 1484; this does not establish rights in modern reproductions.

rights.component: Reported EEBO page images of IB.55085
rights.basis: EEBO is a subscription database; no item-specific reuse license was verified.

rights.component: The 2015 scholarly bibliography and its host-volume PDF
rights.basis: The scholarly article and edited volume were published in 2015; public download availability is not itself a reuse license.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Readings of Harley MS 1764 and corrections accumulated after the first edition
editorial_practices.representation: A revised text incorporating Brock's correction list and renewed proof checking against Harley MS 1764
editorial_practices.description: The revision had distinct contributor scopes: Rossiter supplied the original manuscript transcript and Caxton supplements; Brock compiled corrections to the first edition; an unnamed Clarendon reader transferred them; Fox checked new proofs through p. 48 against Harley; Furnivall checked the remainder, added French-source notes, and wrote the foreword; Munro supplied contents, fresh notes, glossary, phrase/proverb material, and index.
editorial_practices.scope: The full 1906 revised edition, with Fox's manuscript check explicitly limited to pp. 1-48 and Furnivall responsible for the remaining proof check

editorial_practices.source_feature: Caxton's printed pause bars, sparse sentence punctuation, and capitalization
editorial_practices.representation: Pause bars omitted and modernizing stops and capitals supplied
editorial_practices.description: Wright explains that, in the Caxton-derived passages, he omitted the printed pause bars and added stops and capital letters for readability.
editorial_practices.scope: The opening repair and the Caxton-supplied passages on pp. 72-75, 99-101, and 167-205
notes: These editorial punctuation choices should not be attributed to either Harley MS 1764 or Caxton's printed copy.

editorial_practices.source_feature: The complete 1906 revised edition, including contents, text, footnotes, glossary, phrase/proverb list, and index
editorial_practices.representation: Title page, foreword, introduction, edited text through p. 205, and many notes encoded; contents and back matter omitted
editorial_practices.description: The U-M electronic text is a bounded selection from the 1906 volume. Its header's generic claim that front matter and notes were not included is broader than the actual file, which preserves substantial front matter and 102 NOTE1 elements.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Chapter initials and bracketed initial letters in the printed edition
editorial_practices.representation: Chapter initials capitalized and letters supplied in square brackets where recorded by the header
editorial_practices.description: The XML header reports systematic capitalization of chapter initials and bracketed supplied initials.
editorial_practices.scope: Chapter openings throughout the encoded text

editorial_practices.source_feature: Printed hyphenation and line-end word division in the 1906 edition
editorial_practices.representation: Hyphenation and end-of-line division normalized in the electronic text; printed line numbers omitted
editorial_practices.description: The XML header records normalization of hyphenation and end-of-line division and omission of line numbers. It also reports that italic letters are retained as rendition rather than silently flattened.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Humanities Text Initiative, KntTour-L.xml, General Corpus pinned CME submodule copy.
evidence.locator: TEI-style header, source description, revision history, encoding practices, and full document structure
evidence.summary: Identifies the 1997 U-M encoding and Wright's 1906 revised edition as its source; says that edition derives from Harley MS 1764 and Caxton's 1484 print; records normalization and omissions. Direct inspection also establishes exactly what front matter and notes remain in the file.
notes: The file is 492,123 bytes, Git blob b49ee8fef9adaf15a872caa24428c8bc0563d4f9 at CME commit e1d77c88ca561be924b2fedc54dd474be2e7e49c.

evidence.citation: Kyvero Press CME repository, LICENSE and pinned Git state.
evidence.summary: Establishes the exact CME commit used by General Corpus and the repository's Unlicense dedication; the license can apply only to rights controlled by its contributor and does not erase upstream terms.
notes: The LICENSE file is Git blob fdddb29aa445bf3d6a5d843d6dd77e10a9f99657.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Library Digital Collections, The Book of the Knight of La Tour-Landry, KntTour-L.
evidence.summary: Provides the current public digital text and labels the item Public Domain with No Copyright-United States.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Library Digital Collections, Corpus of Middle English Prose and Verse.
evidence.summary: Describes the CME collection and applies the current Public Domain / No Copyright-United States statement at collection level.

evidence.citation: Geoffroy de La Tour-Landry, The Book of the Knight of La Tour-Landry, ed. Thomas Wright, revised edition, EETS Original Series 33 (London, 1906), complete Internet Archive scan.
evidence.locator: title page; Furnivall foreword; Wright introduction; pp. 1, 72-75, 99-101, 167-205; Caxton colophon and ownership inscriptions; contents and back matter
evidence.summary: Primary evidence for the revised edition's contributor roles, manuscript and Caxton source boundaries, editorial practices, pagination, and U-M's bounded selection. Wright's reproduced Lane inscriptions make exact-copy identification possible when joined to de Ricci.
Identifies the hosted item and exact downloadable derivatives; no item-specific reuse license was found in the cached metadata.

evidence.citation: Geoffroy de La Tour-Landry, The Book of the Knight of La Tour-Landry, ed. Thomas Wright, EETS Original Series 33 (London, 1868), complete Internet Archive scan.
evidence.locator: title page, introduction, pp. 1-205, and source notes
evidence.summary: Provides the first-edition state underlying the 1906 revision and confirms Rossiter's manuscript transcription and the passage-level Caxton supplementation.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts Catalogue, Harley MS 1764, The Knight of the Tower.
evidence.locator: Summary, contents ff. 1r-54v, physical description, provenance, and access conditions
evidence.summary: Identifies Harley MS 1764 as the only known copy, dates and localizes the witness, describes its codicology, notes losses after fols. 24 and 32 and its imperfect ending, and gives current consultation requirements.

evidence.citation: British Library, Harley MS 1764, IIIF Presentation 3 manifest.
evidence.locator: 127 canvases; target work on canvases 11-118; metadata 'Usage terms' and manifest rights field
evidence.summary: Provides the complete manuscript facsimile and exact work locators. The manifest says the digitized content is public domain in most countries other than the UK and links to a British Library image-reuse page.
notes: The cached ZIP contains manifest.json, inventory.json, and 127 JPEG files; the inventory records each provider image URL, size, and SHA-256.

evidence.citation: British Library, How to reuse images of unpublished manuscripts (URL embedded in the Harley MS 1764 IIIF manifest).
evidence.summary: The IIIF manifest's designated reuse URL returned HTTP 404 when checked. This is evidence of a broken permission route, not evidence that the manifest's separate non-UK public-domain statement has been withdrawn.

States current British Library website terms, including restrictions concerning commercial use, database creation, and recirculation, and supplies the contractual context that must be kept distinct from copyright status.

evidence.locator: Imaging service, order form, fees, and licensing contact
evidence.summary: Provides the current route for ordering reproductions and directs image-reuse questions to British Library licensing.

evidence.citation: British Library, Register or renew your free Reader Pass.
evidence.locator: Reader Pass eligibility and collection-item consultation
evidence.summary: States that anyone over 18 may register, that a current Reader Pass is required to request collection items, and that physical items are consulted in British Library Reading Rooms.

evidence.citation: CERL Incunabula Short Title Catalogue, il00072000, La Tour-Landry: The knight of the tower, Westminster: William Caxton, 31 January 1484.
evidence.locator: Imprint, references, and British Library holdings
evidence.summary: Confirms the 1484 imprint, ISTC and STC identifiers, and distinguishes IB.55085 from the parallel British Library copy IB.55086. Its legacy digitized-copy link points to the latter copy, not Wright's source.

evidence.citation: Seymour de Ricci, A Census of Caxtons (Oxford: Clarendon Press, 1909), no. 63.
evidence.summary: Identifies the copy bearing Thomas Lane provenance as British Museum IB.55085, formerly C.21.d.15; those ownership inscriptions match the ones Wright prints from his Caxton source.

evidence.citation: Maria Michels-Kohlhage, 'Geoffroy de La Tour Landry: Livre du chevalier de la Tour pour l'enseignements de ses filles - Bibliographie der Druckausgaben und Editionen vom 15. Jahrhundert bis 2015,' in Die Zeitalter werden besichtigt (Frankfurt am Main: Peter Lang, 2015), 141-158.
evidence.locator: printed pp. 141-158, especially Caxton entry p. 144
evidence.summary: Bibliography through 2015; the Caxton entry reports the 1483 translation date and names British Library IB.55085 as the exemplar reproduced by EEBO.

evidence.citation: New York Public Library, Early English Books Online (EEBO) database description.
evidence.summary: Confirms that EEBO provides subscription access to early printed-book page images through subscribing institutions; it does not authenticate the shelfmark of this particular image set.

open_questions.question: How does U-M apply its current Public Domain / No Copyright-United States statement to redistribution in light of the restrictive availability notice embedded in the 1997 XML?
next_steps: Ask U-M Digital Collections which statement governs a new public redistribution of the KntTour-L encoding.
Preserve attribution and the embedded notice until U-M clarifies the conflict.
notes: The current public-domain statement is explicit for the United States; the legacy XML notice expressly forbids redistribution by commercial and nonprofit content providers.

open_questions.question: What permissions or provider terms apply to publishing Harley MS 1764 IIIF images in the United Kingdom or in uses not clearly covered by the manifest's non-UK public-domain assertion?
next_steps: Contact licensing@bl.uk with the IIIF manifest URI, intended repository distribution, print-on-demand context, and target jurisdictions.
Request a current replacement for the manifest's 404 image-reuse page and retain any written permission with the project records.
notes: The broken rights link does not negate the manifest's explicit statement that the images are public domain in most countries other than the UK.

open_questions.question: Can the reported EEBO facsimile of British Library IB.55085 be authenticated at a stable item URL, and is any open complete facsimile of that exact copy available?
next_steps: Search EEBO through a subscribing institution and verify that the image-set shelfmark is IB.55085.
Ask the British Library whether IB.55085 has an unindexed digital surrogate or can be imaged on request.
Do not substitute CERL's legacy link to C.11.c.6 = IB.55086 for Wright's exact source copy.
